annotations.label: Graph of a function
annotations.note: `Set.graphOn f s` is defined as `(fun x ↦ (x, f x)) '' s`, the graph of `f` on `s`.

annotations.label: Graph of a function of two variables

anchor.snippet: the graph usually refers to the set of ordered triples

annotations.note: Specializes `Set.graphOn` to a function `f : α × β → γ`, yielding pairs `((x,y), f (x,y))` equivalent to ordered triples.

annotations.label: Formal definition of graph

anchor.snippet: the graph of the function is the set

annotations.note: `Set.mem_graphOn` characterizes membership in `s.graphOn f` as `x.1 ∈ s ∧ f x.1 = x.2`, matching the formal definition `{(x, f x) | x ∈ X}`.

annotations.label: Graph of a one-variable function

anchor.snippet: is the subset of the set

annotations.note: `Set.graphOn f s : Set (α × β)` is exactly the subset of `α × β` corresponding to the graph of a one-variable function.

annotations.label: Recovering domain and range from the graph
annotations.note: `image_fst_graphOn` shows `Prod.fst '' graphOn f s = s` (domain) and `image_snd_graphOn` gives `Prod.snd '' s.graphOn f = f '' s` (range).

annotations.label: Graph of the cubic polynomial

anchor.snippet: The graph of the cubic polynomial on the real line

annotations.note: No specific declaration formalizes the graph of the particular cubic `x^3 - 9x` as a worked example in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of a trigonometric function of two variables

anchor.snippet: The graph of the trigonometric function

annotations.note: No specific Mathlib declaration captures this particular trigonometric example.

annotations.label: Graph with gradient and level curves

anchor.snippet: such a drawing of the graph of the function

annotations.note: Mathlib has gradients and level sets independently but not this specific worked example pairing them with a graph drawing.
